headlines.title: Monolithic vs. Qualcomm: Which Semiconductor Stock Has More Upside?
headlines.summary: QCOM and MPWR are expanding AI-focused semiconductor portfolios as both pursue growth in data centers, servers and emerging AI markets.

headlines.title: Can Vicor's Licensing Royalties Strengthen Long-Term Growth?
headlines.summary: VICR's expanding licensing strategy could unlock recurring royalty income as AI power demands grow, adding a scalable revenue stream beyond hardware sales.

headlines.title: Earnings Preview: What to Expect From Monolithic Power Systems' Report
headlines.summary: Monolithic Power Systems will release its second-quarter earnings soon, and analysts anticipate a double-digit bottom-line growth.

headlines.title: Does Monolithic Power Systems’ (MPWR) Index Shift Clarify Its Long-Term Competitive Positioning?
headlines.summary: In late June 2026, Monolithic Power Systems, Inc. was added to the Russell Top 200 Index and Russell Top 200 Growth Benchmark while being removed from the Russell Midcap Index and Russell Midcap Growth Benchmark as part of the latest index reconstitution. This shift moves Monolithic Power Systems into a larger-cap benchmark universe, which can alter how institutional investors gain exposure to the stock through index-linked products.

headlines.title: Is Monolithic Power Systems (MPWR) Reasonably Valued Or Too Rich?
headlines.summary: Monolithic Power Systems has delivered a very strong 240.7% return over the past five years, yet its current valuation checks lean expensive. This raises the question of how much of that success is already reflected in the US$1,272.81 share price. A 240.7% gain over five years signals that investors have been willing to pay up for Monolithic Power Systems' growth story, so expectations embedded in the stock are high.

description: Monolithic Power Systems, Inc. provides semiconductor-based power electronics solutions in China, Taiwan, South Korea, Southeast Asia, Europe, the United States, Japan, and internationally. The company offers direct current (DC) to DC solutions to convert and control voltages within a range of electronic systems, such as cloud-based and on-premises CPU servers and workstations, AI systems, memory, storage solutions, notebooks, infotainment, power sources, home appliances, network infrastructure, and satellite communications. It also provides alternating current (AC) to DC; driver metal-oxide-semiconductor field-effect transistors; power management integrated circuits (ICs); and current limit switch and lighting control products. The company serves storage and computing, enterprise data, automotive, communications, consumer, and industrial end markets through third-party distributors and value-added resellers. Monolithic Power Systems, Inc. was incorporated in 1997 and is based in West Palm Beach, Florida.
